chunk_summary: This module outlines structured outpatient visits (two to four) for obesity management, emphasizing measurable progress, lifestyle modules, barrier-solving, and escalating care intensity based on outcomes.
learning_objectives: Understand the importance of sequential outpatient visits in obesity management.
Learn how to deliver lifestyle modules with measurable actions and homework.
Identify and address barriers to treatment adherence effectively.
Apply escalating care intensity based on demonstrated progress and module fidelity.
key_facts: Visits two to four focus on measurable outcomes, including weight, BMI trajectory, and BP monitoring.
Each visit includes one lifestyle module with 2-3 concrete actions and specific homework.
Progress is assessed by trajectory (e.g., weight maintenance with falling BMI in growing children).
Barriers such as exam schedules, unsafe spaces, or family habits require clinical problem-solving.
Escalation from prevention-plus to structured weight management occurs after foundational module fidelity is demonstrated.
reference_quote: Trajectory is more informative than a single value; affirm weight maintenance with falling BMI in growing children as meaningful progress. (AAP Clinical Practice Guideline, 2023)
document_key_topics: Sequential outpatient visits for obesity management
Escalating care intensity based on progress
